content: Hi. Sometimes, pr_info can not print the log due to the print buffer timely. I want to use rust-gdb to get some info. However, when I want to print some local variables using rust-gdb, it always shows "NO symbol foo in the current context". Is there a way to solve this problem?
do you have CONFIG_DEBUG_INFO set to y in your .config?
Yes. I can set breakpoints on the function. But I can not print local variables.

Oh, they're probably optimized out then. You'll probably have to look at the assembly code and try to figure out if the variable happens to still be lying around in some register...

We used to have a config option to set the optimization level of Rust code, but upstream folks didn't like it and I think Miguel was going to remove it, not sure if it's already happened. (If this option is still there, you can disable/lower optimizations and then your variable will become visible.)
Thanks for the explanation! I will check the assembly code to see what's happening.
